description_long: This church features a rectangular stone structure with a prominent bell tower rising from one end, topped with a greenish patina roof and clock faces on each side. The main body of the church is constructed from textured stone blocks, with a steeply pitched black roof covering the nave. Arched windows and doorways are distributed along the sides and ends, with a larger arched entrance at the front. The bell tower is square and rises above the main roofline, capped with a distinctive green roof and small arched openings for the bell. Two small detached structures, possibly gatehouses or chapels, are positioned near the main building. The overall appearance is robust and historic, with architectural details suggesting a Romanesque influence.
description: A stone church with a tall bell tower, arched windows, and a steep black roof, accompanied by two small outbuildings. The structure has a historic, Romanesque appearance with a green patina roof on the tower.
description_view_0: Side view showing the long stone nave, black roof, and the bell tower with its green roof; two small outbuildings are visible to the right.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the main arched entrance, the full height of the bell tower, and the symmetrical facade; outbuildings are in the foreground.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the length of the nave, a secondary arched entrance, and the bell tower; outbuildings are to the left.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back wall with a large arched window, the steep roof, and the bell tower rising above; only one outbuilding is visible.
